# UC-23.5.2 · Inventory Level Monitoring and Stockout Risk

## Description

Consumes point-in-time ERP/WMS inventory snapshots ingested as `dbx:inventory`, aligns SKU keys across naming conventions with `coalesce()`, computes days-of-cover after subtracting policy safety stock from salable quantity, applies tiered `case()` severity (`STOCKOUT`, `CRITICAL_BELOW_LEAD`, `REORDER_NOW`, `AT_RISK`, `OVERSTOCK`, `HEALTHY`), attaches dollar exposure using forward-looking revenue-at-risk during replenishment gaps for the worst tiers, aggregates portfolio totals with `eventstats`, and suppresses healthy rows so planners receive an actionable warehouse grain exception table—not cycle-time histograms across sales-document stages.

## Value

Supply-chain controllers and purchasing managers gain an objective picture of where demand could outrun replenishment before bins empty or promotions crater service levels; treasury stakeholders see monetized exposure tied to velocity and procurement calendars rather than anecdotal warehouse callbacks; SIOP forums inherit defensible SKU lists grounded in JDBC-fed quantities comparable to SAP MB52/MMBE reconciliations so debates shift from arguing spreadsheets to sequencing buys and reroutes across regions.

## Implementation

(1) Publish a JDBC DB Connect batch input emitting `dbx:inventory` rows into `index=business` with rising watermark on snapshot freshness and bounded thirty-minute pulls; (2) align SAP `MATNR`, Oracle item IDs, or NetSuite internal IDs into consistent searchable SKU aliases upstream or via SPL coalesce; (3) compute thirty-day consumption averages in SQL if Splunk must remain thin; (4) save the bundled SPL as an hourly alert excluding HEALTHY rows and attach procurement routing plus Dashboard Studio tiles showing portfolio_at_risk.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Splunk Enterprise or Splunk Cloud search tier aligned with splunkVersions below; Heavy Forwarder or dedicated Splunk DB Connect worker reachable across JDBC to SAP HANA (`jdbc:sap://host:3NN15`), Oracle RAC (`jdbc:oracle:thin:@//SCAN:1521/service`), SQL Server warehouse lanes, or NetSuite SuiteAnalytics Connect endpoints approved by InfoSec.
• Splunk DB Connect identities storing least-privilege JDBC credentials restricted to read-only projections exposing inventory snapshots—not SYSDBA or SAP DDIC passwords surfaced inside Splunk roles.
• Supply-chain consensus on reorder-point computation versus planner-maintained SAP MD04 outputs so JDBC numeric columns reconcile with ERP rounding policies.
• Splunk RBAC granting roles `srchIndexesAllowed` covering `business` while restricting sensitive valuation exports unless CFO governance grants broader visibility.
• Capacity planning awareness that thirty-minute polls generating tens of thousands of SKUs land roughly twice hourly—budget license throughput accordingly versus hourly SAP BW extracts.

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Stand up DB Connect inputs targeting curated warehouse-ready SQL rather than raw `MSEG` replay loops the UC avoids ingesting movement journals wholesale; representative stanza naming `[db://hana/rs_inventory_snapshot]` merges unrestricted quantities analogous to SAP table `MARD`.`LABST`, valuation helpers (`MBEW`-style rolling averages when Finance publishes standard prices per valuation area), planner fields (`MARC`-linked procurement calendars expressed as integer day offsets), plus enrichment columns (`abc_class`) emitted from segmentation logic maintained by Analytics Engineering.

Emit sourcetype `dbx:inventory` into `index=business`; configure props macros ensuring `_time` maps from warehouse snapshot timestamps (`last_updated`) so dashboards reconcile against SAP GUI comparisons executed via MM inventory displays MB52 stock overview per material and plant, MB5B stock at posting date when auditors inspect retro balances, MMBE multi-level stock determination spanning plants.

Oracle environments expose views layering `MTL_ONHAND_QUANTITIES_DETAIL` quantities filtered by subinventory segments representing reserve versus pick-face staging—mirror those constructs directly inside SQL instead of rebuilding BOM explosions downstream.

NetSuite deployments relying on SuiteAnalytics surfaces approximate REST payloads proxied through JDBC—ensure numeric columns mirror SuiteScript inventory detail endpoints returning quantity-on-hand plus reorder thresholds documented under SuiteAnswers inventory administration references.

Parameterize thirty-minute intervals (`interval = 1800`) plus rising predicates tied to watermark expressions (`WHERE last_updated > ?`) bound by Splunk lookahead macros—prevent duplicate floods during ERP replay maintenance windows Basis schedules quarterly.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Paste the SPL packaged alongside this UC—clone before attaching procurement-specific thresholds (`lead_time_days` guardrails stay encoded inline via fourteen-day fallback yet enterprises should replace literals with lookups keyed by commodity lane).

Schedule hourly cron (`0 * * * *`) evaluating `-24h@h` freshness covering delayed JDBC stalls—route STOCKOUT plus CRITICAL_BELOW_LEAD rows immediately via webhook into procure-to-pay tooling while OVERSTOCK segments digest weekly Markdown clearance cadences owned by Revenue Growth Management.

Throttle alerts per `(sku_key,warehouse_id)` tuple across six-hour spans—suppress churn during overlapping ERP clones migrating ECC warehouses toward SAP S/4HANA templates.

Step 3 — Pipeline mechanics
The opening clause binds Splunk events to recent JDBC arrivals inside twenty-four-hour rolling windows ensuring stale snapshots remain visible until investigations conclude rather than disappearing silently—critical because nightly ERP batches sometimes freeze postings earlier than Manhattan WMS telemetry reflects pallet scans.

`coalesce()` collapses SKU identifiers spanning lowercase ERP exports (`sku`), SAP alphanumeric material numbers (`material`), retailer merchandise hierarchies (`item_number`), and Salesforce CPQ surrogate IDs (`ProductId`)—prevent duplicate warehouse rows confusing downstream procurement pivots.

The stats aggregation collapses duplicate JDBC retries carrying identical watermark timestamps—`latest()` retains freshest monetary valuations (`unit_price`) while grouping strictly by SKU plus warehouse identifiers reflecting Phoenix versus Atlanta segmentation discussed later.

Subtracting safety stock from gross quantity before dividing by trailing consumption anchors inventory planners treating SS buckets as non-negotiable buffers—the derived `doc_safe` denominator expresses salable coverage aligned with reorder formulas leveraging service-level policy tables maintained externally.

`case()` evaluates forward-looking replenishment posture instead of reconstructing historic dwell intervals across journey stages—the STOCKOUT branch fires prior to evaluating reorder thresholds whenever unrestricted quantities exhaust regardless whether ERP simultaneously raises inbound ASN optimism elsewhere.

`revenue_at_risk` monetizes replenishment-cycle vulnerability strictly when replenishment lag intersects nonzero velocity—Finance prefers multiplying average selling prices sourced from valuation masters rather than promotional spike mixes tangential here.

`eventstats` layers portfolio totals atop filtered exceptions keeping purchasers mindful how discrete crises accumulate versus localized bubbles.

The concluding table emphasizes actionable procurement descriptors (`abc_class`) encouraging differentiated chase tactics rather than treating SKUs uniformly.

Step 4 — Validate
Compare with SAP MB52 inventory dashboard columns (unrestricted-use stock per plant) when validating Splunk rows: open `/nMB52`, filter the same material and storage location tuples returned by Splunk, and read the unrestricted quantity line while accounting for quality inspection (`INSME`) or blocked (`SPEME`) buckets that JDBC may omit unless the projection explicitly merges them.

Compare with Oracle Inventory organization subinventory summaries when JDBC ingests only `MTL_ONHAND_QUANTITIES_DETAIL` slices: the web UI Inventory Balances inquiry should match sum-of-subinventory math after you apply the identical organization, subinventory, and locator filters encoded in SQL.

Compare with NetSuite SuiteAnalytics workbook inventory balance panels (or Record browser item detail) for the matching `itemId` and `locationId` pair—SuiteScript-driven `getInventory` style JSON often includes committed and on-order buckets not present in Splunk exports, so label dashboard footnotes whenever the UC intentionally limits to on-hand unrestricted numbers only.

Perform additional drill hours during pilot weeks: pull five Splunk tuples flagged CRITICAL_BELOW_LEAD, locate identical plant and material identifiers inside SAP MB52, verify unrestricted quantities align within rounding tolerance versus JDBC decimals exported using SAP numeric formatting conventions, and escalate to Basis if HANA rounding profiles differ from BI tool expectations.

Cross-foot Oracle Fusion or EBS dashboards referencing inventory valuation totals versus JDBC snapshots, ensuring duplicate rows representing hub-spoke transfers net appropriately before trusting regional roll-ups.

NetSuite drills should confirm SuiteAnalytics totals reconcile against Items inventory dashboard quantities filtered identically—differences frequently stem from subsidiary toggles omitted accidentally inside JDBC predicates.

Track `_indextime - _time` skew statistics weekly because JDBC outage replay bursts inflate backlog counters unrelated to replenishment posture, and audit whether parallel SAP ECC versus S/4HANA cutover landscapes momentarily dual-write divergent `MARD` extracts into the warehouse before decommissioning legacy systems.

Step 5 — Operational caveats and known false positives
• ERP costing reruns occasionally blank trailing averages overnight leaving JDBC snapshots with missing fields until dawn replenishment batches finish—pause paging while Splunk duplicate-count audits prove cardinality versus SAP MB52 exports rather than trusting silent HEALTHY afternoons alone.
• Snapshot clocks diverge when ERP nightly batches finalize quantities hours before Manhattan WMS acknowledges pallet scans—the same SKU appears adequately stocked inside Splunk yet warehouse supervisors observe bare pick faces until reconciliation completes.
• In-transit PO quantities awaiting SAP goods receipt postings inflate STOCKOUT perceptions whenever ASN timestamps promise arrivals sooner than procurement master lead-time constants baked into JDBC snapshots.
• Rolling thirty-day consumption ignores hockey-stick uplift tied to seasonal calendars—school-supply ramps or calendar-Q4 gifting shrink computed coverage versus planners augmenting buys offline.
• Aggregating enterprise totals collapses geographically separated DC pairs each retaining localized safety cushions—Splunk totals compare poorly unless planners mentally allocate buffers east versus west.
• Month-end cycle-count corrections ±5–10 percent tweak unrestricted quantities absent genuine shipments until planners annotate ERP journals linking variance codes.
• Reverse-logistics floods recycle inventory faster than trailing averages anticipate—returns postings swell unrestricted balances without proportional outbound velocity refreshes feeding averages simultaneously.

## SPL

```spl
index=business sourcetype="dbx:inventory" earliest=-24h@h latest=now()
| eval sku_key=trim(tostring(coalesce(sku, item_number, material, ProductId, "")))
| eval warehouse_id=tostring(coalesce(warehouse_id, plant, warehouse, location_id, "UNSPECIFIED"))
| eval qty_raw=tonumber(coalesce(qty_on_hand, on_hand_qty, quantity_on_hand, "0"))
| eval dd=tonumber(coalesce(daily_demand_avg, daily_demand_avg_30d, avg_daily_demand, "0"))
| eval rp=tonumber(coalesce(reorder_point, reorder_pt, reorder_level, "0"))
| eval lt=tonumber(coalesce(lead_time_days, supplier_lead_days, procurement_lt_days, "0"))
| eval ss=tonumber(coalesce(safety_stock_qty, safety_stock, ss_qty, "0"))
| eval unit_price=tonumber(coalesce(unit_price, standard_price, moving_avg_price, "0"))
| where len(sku_key)>0
| stats latest(qty_raw) as qty_on_hand latest(dd) as daily_demand_avg latest(rp) as reorder_point latest(lt) as lead_time_days latest(ss) as safety_stock_qty latest(unit_price) as unit_price latest(item_name) as item_name latest(abc_class) as abc_class by sku_key warehouse_id
| eval lead_time_days=if(isnull(lead_time_days) OR lead_time_days<=0, 14, lead_time_days)
| eval qty_cov=max(qty_on_hand-safety_stock_qty, 0)
| eval doc_safe=if(daily_demand_avg>0, round(qty_cov/daily_demand_avg, 3), null())
| eval status=case(
    qty_on_hand<=0, "STOCKOUT",
    daily_demand_avg>0 AND isnotnull(doc_safe) AND doc_safe < lead_time_days AND qty_on_hand>0, "CRITICAL_BELOW_LEAD",
    qty_on_hand < reorder_point, "REORDER_NOW",
    daily_demand_avg>0 AND isnotnull(doc_safe) AND doc_safe >= lead_time_days AND doc_safe < lead_time_days+14 AND qty_on_hand >= reorder_point, "AT_RISK",
    daily_demand_avg>0 AND isnotnull(doc_safe) AND doc_safe > 180, "OVERSTOCK",
    1=1, "HEALTHY")
| eval revenue_at_risk=if(status IN ("STOCKOUT","CRITICAL_BELOW_LEAD"), daily_demand_avg*unit_price*lead_time_days, 0)
| where status!="HEALTHY"
| eventstats sum(revenue_at_risk) as portfolio_at_risk
| sort - revenue_at_risk sku_key
| table sku_key warehouse_id item_name qty_on_hand safety_stock_qty reorder_point daily_demand_avg lead_time_days doc_safe status revenue_at_risk portfolio_at_risk abc_class
```

## Visualization

Dashboard Studio layout: upper ribbon single-value tiles for STOCKOUT count and CRITICAL_BELOW_LEAD sum(revenue_at_risk); sortable splunk.table of sku_key × warehouse_id exception rows including portfolio_at_risk column for enterprise roll-up context; optional trellis bar chart by abc_class aggregating filtered rows; supplemental choropleth only when geo-coded warehouses exist.
